name: PEGIN Knowledge Base
description: Decentralized SSO on Chia + DIG Network. Canonical markdown for AI/RAG.

exclude_from_rag.reason: Navigation, HTML-only, consolidated duplicate, or archived content.

documents.title: PEGIN developer documentation
documents.summary: **Start here if you build or integrate PEGIN.** Specs, architecture, SDK, and protocol reference live in this folder. Vision, product narrative, and business docs are elsewhere — linked below.

documents.title: Architecture
documents.summary: System design for identity on **Chia** and data on **DIG**.

documents.title: PEGIN + DIG Network: Enterprise Application Architecture Transformation
documents.summary: > **The Insight:** PEGIN (authentication + permissions) + DIG Network (data) solve the infrastructure layer entirely. Business logic stays the same. But how applications are built fundamentally changes.

documents.title: DIG Network Incentives + PEGIN DSSO Integration
documents.summary: > **Direction:** DIG stores PEGIN identity data; peers may earn network incentives (e.g. XCH) for storage and proofs. Customer and operator outcomes must be modeled per deployment.

documents.title: PEGIN On-Chain Architecture
documents.summary: *Technical architecture — blockchain-only design (Chia + optional DIG)*

documents.title: Integration
documents.summary: Integrate **Login with PEGIN** in your application.

documents.title: Specifications
documents.summary: Implementation specs for PEGIN engineers. Read **Spec 1** before writing code; read **Spec 2** before enterprise protocol work.

documents.title: Spec 2 — Enterprise identity (AD / Entra parity)
documents.summary: > **Goal:** PEGIN as a decentralized IdP that can **federate with** or **replace** Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) for app SSO — using **standard protocols only** (no proprietary Azure APIs required for SAML/OIDC apps).

documents.title: Spec 1 — PEGIN technical stack (Chia + POC)
documents.summary: > **PEGIN** = **P**enguin **G**ateway **I**de**n**tity — decentralized SSO on Chia + DIG.

documents.title: Structural differentiators vs centralized SSO
documents.summary: > **Purpose:** Explain what a user-owned DID enables that traditional IdPs are not designed to do.

documents.title: POC & MVP — Login with PEGIN
documents.summary: User → "Login with PEGIN" → passkey (WebAuthn) → Chia DID verified → JWT → app

documents.title: PEGIN product roadmap
documents.summary: > Single plan for what we build, in what order, and what is explicitly **future**. Timelines are planning estimates until the POC ships.

documents.title: PEGIN Complete Ecosystem — SSO + Permission Management
documents.summary: PEGIN combines **passwordless SSO** (user-held DID) with a planned **permission layer (PePP)** on DIG. This doc describes how the pieces fit; metrics come from future pilots.

documents.title: PEGIN Permission Platform (PePP) — Decentralized Access Control
documents.summary: > **Phase:** [Phase 2 in roadmap](../03-use-cases/roadmap.md) — not part of POC.

documents.title: PEGIN — Timeless Business Principles & Customer Value Proposition
documents.summary: These principles transcend technology trends, market cycles, and management fads. They work because they align incentives: the business wins when customers win.

documents.title: PEGIN's Core Value: Your Login Belongs To You, Not Your Company
documents.summary: > **The Core Truth:** Your login should belong to YOU, not your employer. PEGIN makes this real.

documents.title: PEGIN — Fully Decentralized
documents.summary: *Enterprise SSO + custody on Chia and DIG (no central servers)*

documents.title: PEGIN Business Plan
documents.summary: **PEGIN** (Penguin Gateway Identity) is decentralized single sign-on on Chia + optional DIG storage: passkey login anchored to a user-owned DID, with credentials and verification on-chain or via open protocols—not a hosted multi-tenant IdP.

documents.title: PEGIN competitive positioning
documents.summary: PEGIN does not win by quoting a lower **per-seat price** than Okta or Entra before anyone has run a pilot. It wins — if it wins — on **how identity is owned, deployed, and governed**.

documents.title: PEGIN Enterprise Business Plan
documents.summary: Enterprise-grade decentralized SSO — **planning doc**. Competes with Azure AD / Okta at the **app SSO layer** (SAML/OIDC/SCIM), not full Active Directory in v1. No validated pricing or growth figures.

documents.title: PEGIN sustainable funding model
documents.summary: PEGIN targets **open-source core** (no per-seat license as the default story) and revenue from **infrastructure, services, and ecosystem participation** — not from locking customers into annual seat tax.

documents.title: PEGIN Strategic Positioning: Additive to Chia Network, Not Competitive
documents.summary: Chia Network Inc's Focus (2025-2026):

documents.title: PEGIN vs Notbot: Complementary Not Competitive
documents.summary: > **Notbot:** "Prove you're human (not a bot or deepfake)"

documents.title: Penguin Gateway — Business Plan
documents.summary: *Decentralized SSO and identity access layer (Chia + DIG)*

documents.title: Penguin Vault — Business Plan
documents.summary: **Penguin Vault** is a planned decentralized identity and key-custody layer on Chia + DIG: serverless authentication, user-controlled vaults, and optional institutional custody (KCS) with timelocked recovery—not a centralized password vault.

documents.title: PEGIN documentation
documents.summary: PEGIN (Penguin Gateway Identity) is decentralized SSO on Chia + DIG. **If you build or integrate PEGIN, start with [08-developer/README.md](08-developer/README.md)** — that is the primary technical documentation.
